The Social Network (2010)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $96,962,694Details
International Box Office $127,959,441
Worldwide Box Office $224,922,135
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $22,832,388
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $11,910,230
Total $34,742,618
Further financial details...

Synopsis

On a fall night in 2003, Harvard undergrad and computer programming genius Mark Zuckerberg sits down at his computer and heatedly begins working on a new idea. In a fury of blogging and programming, what begins in his dorm room soon becomes a global social network and a revolution in communication. A mere six years and 500 million friends later, Mark Zuckerberg is the youngest billionaire in history... but for this entrepreneur, success leads to both personal and legal complications.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$22,445,653 (23.1% of total gross)
Legs:4.32 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:43.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$40,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,771 opening theaters/2,921 max. theaters, 7.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $146,951,600

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 1st, 2010 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: November 11th, 2010 (Wide) (New Zealand)
November 18th, 2010 (Wide) (South Korea)
November 12th, 2020 (Wide) (Australia)
June 9th, 2022 (Limited) (New Zealand)
Video Release: January 11th, 2011 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sexual content, drug and alcohol use and language.
(Rating bulletin 2138, 9/15/2010)
Running Time: 120 minutes
Award nomination: 2011 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Drama, Writers Guild of America Best Adapted Screenplay Winner
Cast, crew, or production detail: Same Actor, Multiple Roles
Plot point: Boss From Hell, Internet, Twins
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures, Relativity Media
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

On a fall night in 2003, Harvard undergrad and computer programming genius Mark Zuckerberg sits down at his computer and heatedly begins working on a new idea. In a fury of blogging and programming, what begins in his dorm room soon becomes a global social network and a revolution in communication. A mere six years and 500 million friends later, Mark Zuckerberg is the youngest billionaire in history... but for this entrepreneur, success leads to both personal and legal complications.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$22,445,653 (23.1% of total gross)
Legs:4.32 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:43.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$40,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,771 opening theaters/2,921 max. theaters, 7.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $146,951,600

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 1st, 2010 (Wide) by Sony Pictures

November 12th, 2020 (Wide) (Australia)
June 9th, 2022 (Limited) (New Zealand)
Video Release: January 11th, 2011 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sexual content, drug and alcohol use and language.
(Rating bulletin 2138, 9/15/2010)
